bead.title: expand microsite concepts docs from refined frame
bead.description:
V2 of 3 children under epic ddx-53c96967. Depends on V1 (ddx-c5dce902).

Port the refined product-vision.md and principles.md into user-facing microsite docs at website/content/docs/concepts/.

CURRENT STATE:
- website/content/docs/_index.md and getting-started.md exist.
- website/content/docs/concepts/ exists (check current contents — may have stubs).
- website/content/docs/cli/, ecosystem/, server/ also exist.

AUDIENCE DIFFERENCE FROM FRAME DOCS:
- Frame docs (docs/helix/) are for contributors and AI agents — terse, decision-focused.
- Microsite docs are for users discovering DDx — needs introduction, examples, why-this-not-that comparisons.

REQUIRED PAGES (create or refine):
1. concepts/_index.md or overview — what is DDx, three-layer stack, how DDx/HELIX/Dun differ. Pull from refined vision.
2. concepts/principles.md — the load-bearing principles (from refined principles.md), with brief examples of each in practice.
3. concepts/architecture.md — concrete: bead lifecycle, execute-loop, persona/role binding, project-local install model. Reference (don't duplicate) FEAT-* spec IDs.
4. concepts/glossary.md — key terms (bead, harness, persona, plugin, project-local, etc.) with one-line definitions.

Voice: clear, friendly but technical. Hugo with Hextra theme. Use Hextra components where they help (callouts, tabs).

OUT OF SCOPE: redesigning the microsite layout, changing the theme, generating visuals (V3 handles those — leave placeholders like '<!-- diagram: three-layer-stack -->' where they belong).
bead.acceptance: 1. website/content/docs/concepts/ contains an overview/index, principles, architecture, glossary pages (create or refine). 2. Three-layer stack (DDx/HELIX/Dun) explained early. 3. Each principle has a brief in-practice example. 4. Architecture page covers: bead lifecycle, execute-loop with review, persona binding, project-local install model. 5. Glossary defines: bead, harness, persona, plugin, project-local, execute-loop, three-layer stack, at minimum. 6. Hugo build succeeds: cd website && hugo --quiet (if hugo available) or theme rendering check. 7. Diagram placeholders use HTML comments, e.g. <!-- diagram: bead-lifecycle -->, so V3 can fill them in.
